title: Stakeholder Outcomes, Owner Authorization, Ethics, and Evidence · Independent Type Trial
summary: An independent municipal service owner supplies a different origin, markup family, dynamic boundary, accessibility need, resource budget, and injected failure and transfers stakeholder outcomes, owner authorization, ethics, and evidence into a changed authorized inclusive site-audit charter. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of authorized inclusive site-audit charter with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, chang…
objectives: Define stakeholder, affected users, decision, site population, success, failure harm, and observable value before choosing crawler features.
Record who controls each target, permitted identities, paths, methods, times, data uses, and revocation contact before any automated request.
Separate RFC 9309 crawl preferences from contracts, copyright, privacy, jurisdiction, and qualified legal review.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Lab Stakeholder Decision
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab TypeScript team handles case 1119 during an isolated browser, package, and restore smoke. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to gate useful owner-authorized, privacy-minimized, inclusive crawl outcomes and claim layers; every external value remains unknown until runtime admission produces an owned value; then inject one authenticated, private-data, output-injection, path, log, plugin, or correction fault and contain it. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Define stakeholder, affected users, decision, site population, success, failure harm, and observable value before choosing crawler features.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Lab Stakeholder Decision.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for define stakeholder, affected users, decision, site population, success, failure harm, and observable value before choosing crawler features. For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Lab Stakeholder Decision, keep Stakeholder Decision connected to executable TypeScript authorization, URL, runtime admission, Fetch, Promise, browser, accessibility, report, package, and recovery decision evidence.
